items.name: Item: Content Page
items.text: Listen to the story and then answer the questions
items.tts: A small boy walks to a vending machine. He puts money into the machine and buys a can of coke. Then he puts money into the machine again, and buys another can of coke. Finally, without drinking them, he puts both cans on the ground. He stands with one foot on each. Then he puts money into the machine again. This time he buys a can of pepsi, which he had always wanted. But it had been too high to reach.

items.name: Item: Passage GapFill
items.instructions: Fill in each of the missing words.
items.passage: A small boy walks to a [vending] machine. He puts [money] into the machine and buys a can of [coke]. Then he puts money into the machine again, and buys another can of coke. Finally, without drinking them, he puts both cans on the [ground]. He stands with one foot on each. Then he puts money into the machine again. This time he buys a can of [pepsi], which he had always wanted. But it had been too [high] to reach.

items.name: Answer the question I (multichoice + image)
items.text: What does the boy do with the two cans of coke at the end?
answers: He puts them on the ground and stands on them.
He gives them to a friend.

items.name: Watch the Video (content page)
items.text: Good Job!  Now watch the video.

items.name: Item: Free Writing
items.instructions: Type your answer to the question in the text area below.
items.text: What do you think the boy will do with the two coke cans next? Why do you think so?
items.freewritingtopic: A small boy walks to a vending machine. He puts money into the machine and buys a can of coke. Then he puts money into the machine again, and buys another can of coke. Finally, without drinking them, he puts both cans on the ground. He stands with one foot on each. Then he puts money into the machine again. This time he buys a can of pepsi, which he had always wanted. But it had been too high to reach.
items.aigradeinstructions: Deduct 1 point for each grammar, spelling or punctuation error. Give 1 point for each full sentence that has no mistakes.
